exemplar.title: Modeling the flow of water from a burette

detailedExemplarData.title: Modeling the flow of water from a burette

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear sequential sections with logical progression from introduction through analysis to conclusion
Effective use of headings, contents table and page numbers to support navigation
Concise final summary that highlights the outcome and relevance of the quadratic model
weaknesses: Occasional grammatical lapses and minor logical jumps (e.g., confusing displacement with height)
Some tables lack captions and unit notation, and run over pages
Repetitive background explanations and lengthy descriptions dilute overall conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed, with clear macro-structure (band 3 for coherence and organization). Although most material is relevant, repetitive tool descriptions and excessive background push it below full conciseness (band 2). Overall performance aligns with a band 3 mark.

strengths: Consistent use of correct symbols, differential notation and function names
Appropriate tables, scatter plots and fitted curves that aid understanding
Generally clear explanatory prose that moves logically from data to model selection
weaknesses: Occasional missing units and inconsistent variable notation (Î½/v confusion)
Axis labels omitted on some figures and no residual plots
Abrupt transitions in derivations and missing explicit display of key equations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly consistent with only minor slips (band 3), the multiple representations are effective but not exhaustive (band 3), and the logical structure is good though transitions sometimes lack justification (band 3). These justify a score of 3.

strengths: Investigation topic and methodology demonstrate significant independent thinking
First-person narrative and reflective comments show a clear personal approach
Use of Logger Pro video analysis and analytical derivation shows good creativity
weaknesses: Methodology follows standard practice with limited innovation
Some repetition in aim statement reduces impact of personal introduction
Modelling tools employed are fairly typical, limiting exceptional initiative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking and a clear personal approach, with good creativity and initiative, placing it squarely in the middle band (band 2).

strengths: Thoughtful comparison of models with qualitative discussion of over-/under-estimation
Meaningful identification of apparatus and software limitations with suggested improvements
Concrete forward-planning through proposals for geometric detail and resistance factors
weaknesses: Reflections lack quantitative residual or uncertainty analysis
No systematic error quantification or propagation
Broader implications and future experimental designs are not deeply probed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflections are meaningful and clearly evaluate outcomes with thoughtful analysis (band 2), but fail to include deeper critical insight or quantitative error evaluation necessary for higher bands.

strengths: Use of linear, quadratic, logarithmic regressions and ODE solution directly informs conclusions
Differential-equation approach and curve fitting are fully commensurate with course level
Correct algebraic manipulations underpin the derived model and comparisons
weaknesses: Mix of cgs and SI units and omission of negative sign in outflow equation indicate inconsistencies
No residual analysis or uncertainty quantification to assess model fit
Minor symbol notation errors and missing absolute-value in integration step
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevant mathematics is applied appropriately (band 4), and the level is commensurate (band 4), but understanding and accuracy exhibit enough minor errors to align with band 3 overall. Hence a score of 3 aligns with the evidence.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanatory prose is generally clear and moves logically from data to choice of model to comparison with theory.  Some transitions are abrupt and derivations are not always fully justified, but overall coherence and clarity meet the âgood logical structureâ standard.

Algebraic manipulation and separation are handled correctly; fitted and derived models match the data reasonably well.  Nonetheless, unit inconsistency (cm vs m), mistakes in parameter symbols and absence of residual or uncertainty analysis reveal minor inaccuracies, placing understanding in the âgoodâ¦ mostly correctâ category rather than âthorough and correctâ.

The student provides tables, scatter plots, fitted curves and algebraic models.  These multiple representations are appropriate and help convey meaning; however, axis labels/units are occasionally missing and residual plots are absent, preventing the âhighly effectiveâ descriptor.

The report is separated into clear sequential sections (Introduction â Data â Analysis â Conclusion) and the mathematical argument builds from raw data through candidate-generated models to a final evaluation. Minor digressions, occasional grammatical lapses and a few logical jumps (e.g., confusing displacement with height) prevent the narrative from being âhighly coherentâ, but overall the line of reasoning is easy to follow.

Initiative is shown by employing Logger Pro video analysis and attempting an analytical derivation, but the modelling tools and mathematics remain fairly typical of HL coursework, so creativity is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.

The student compares models, discusses over-/under-estimation and explains why the quadratic fit is superior.  Analysis is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulââno quantitative residual analysis or uncertainty propagation is carried out.

Outcomes are evaluated qualitatively (e.g., derived model empties at 33.5 s) and limitations are acknowledged with suggestions for improvement, but there is no systematic error analysis or discussion of broader implications, matching the âclear evaluationâ descriptor.

Three discrete reflection sections identify limitations of the apparatus, software and modelling assumptions, showing more than a token comment yet not probing deeply into alternative experimental designs or theoretical implications.  This aligns with âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ.

Correct symbols, differential notation and function names are used consistently and units are usually stated.  Minor slips (Î½/v confusion, missing absolute-value bars in the integral step) occur, so the work fits âmostly consistentâ rather than completely flawless usage.

The write-up is written in the first person, includes personal reflections and design decisions, and clearly shows a personal route through the task.  However, methodology largely follows standard practice and lacks strong innovation, placing it in the middle band.

Most material is directly related to modelling the burette flow, yet repetitive explanations of Torricelliâs law, lengthy tool descriptions and word-heavy reflections inflate the length.  Hence the exploration is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than fully concise.

Linear, quadratic, logarithmic regressions plus an analytically derived ODE solution are all relevant to the context and used purposefully.  The mathematics clearly informs conclusions, evidencing ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ; however, advanced techniques such as numerical methods, error bounds or optimisation are absent, so it does not reach the comprehensive (5) or expert (6) levels.

Selecting the investigation topic, designing the experiment and choosing to compare empirical and theoretical models demonstrate the studentâs own thinking beyond textbook examples, yet the modelling strategy (quadratic/log) is fairly conventional, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Headings, a contents table and page numbers provide a clear macroscopic structure; figures and tables appear close to the text that references them.  While some tables run over pages without captions and several figures are poorly labelled, the overall organisation supports the reader effectively, justifying the âwell organised with clear structureâ level rather than âexceptionally well organisedâ.

The differential-equation approach, separation of variables and regression work are fully commensurate with HL AA expectations, but they stop short of higher-level extensions such as non-separable ODEs or multivariable calculus, so the strand sits at âfully commensurateâ (4).

annotations.comment: The table of contents spans a large area; consider adjusting spacing and alignment to improve readability and coherence.
Multiple entries for âConclusionâ and âEvaluationâ may confuse the reader; streamline headings to maintain a clear structure.
The personal introduction demonstrates genuine engagement, but the aim statement repeats itself; condense to avoid unnecessary repetition.
Variables in the equation should be introduced inline before or immediately after the block; ensure $$v=\sqrt{2gh}$$ is preceded by clear definitions.
The background paragraph is informative but overly long; consider splitting complex sentences and focusing on key concepts.
Describing camera placement to reduce parallax shows thoughtful experimental design and enhances data integrity.
The photograph lacks a scale bar or dimension annotation; include a clear reference to translate pixel measurements to physical units.
Constant notation switches between uppercase \(C\) and lowercase \(c\); maintain one symbol to avoid ambiguity.
The first reflection insightfully identifies setup issues, demonstrating meaningful consideration of experimental design.
The analysis introduction clearly states the use of GeoGebra for curve fitting, reflecting sound mathematical communication.
The description of polynomial flexibility is well articulated, effectively linking theory to model choice.
The transition to model fitting is abrupt; add a linking sentence explaining why starting with a linear model establishes a useful baseline.
The term âv interceptâ is incorrect; replace with ây-interceptâ to use standard mathematical terminology.
Include units for slope and intercept in the caption (e.g., cm/s and cm) to clarify the dimensions of Geogebraâs line parameters.
Critique of the linear model is clear but would benefit from a quantitative residual or error analysis to support the claims.
Reflection acknowledges several limitations but does not quantify their impact; consider estimating the magnitude of error introduced by each.
Raw data table is comprehensive but lacks a caption and clear unit notation in the header; adding these will improve presentation.
Explain the rationale for rounding to four significant figures explicitly; this clarifies the connection between measurement precision and data processing.
Time values stop at 24.5 s; consider discussing why the experiment ends there or extending to full emptying to validate model predictions.
The quadratic model is praised but its explicit form isnât displayed in the text; include the equation for completeness.
The logarithmic model uses \(\ln(x)\) but the independent variable is time \(t\); revise to \(h(t)=a\ln(t)+b\) for consistency.
Reflection text on this page ends mid-sentence; ensure all sentences are complete for clarity and professionalism.
Symbols âÎ½â and âvâ are used interchangeably for velocity; maintain consistent notation to avoid confusion.
In the outflow rate equation, include a negative sign explicitly when defining \(dV/dt\) to denote volume loss over time.
Using 980 for \(g\) in cm/sÂ² mixes unit systems; clarify if all calculations use cgs or SI consistently.
Integration step omits absolute-value notation for \(h\); include \(\int h^{-1/2}\,dh\) justification to ensure mathematical rigor.
The final function uses \(\Delta h\) but earlier context defines \(h\); clarify variables so the reader tracks what is being modeled.
Discuss the percentage error or residuals when comparing predicted emptying time to observed behavior to deepen critical analysis.
Reflection on derivation challenges exhibits substantial critical thinking and acknowledges modeling trade-offs.
Two separate âConclusionâ headings appear; consolidate to prevent structural confusion.
The final summary succinctly states the outcome and relevance of the quadratic model, enhancing the readerâs takeaway.
Final reflection proposes concrete improvements (geometric detail, resistance factors), showing thoughtful forward-planning.
Comparing empirical and derived curves is insightful; include a quantitative goodness-of-fit metric (e.g., RÂ²) to strengthen analysis.
The citation of âChatgptâ lacks the author or retrieval date format; verify source credibility and adhere to academic conventions.
